H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) treats over‑stimulation via DESCENT. Qualitative inputs (n=29) emphasize noticeable improvement (~66% positive) and quick settling (5‑10 min), with strong music appreciation. YouTube reach is modest (30 views) but lacks retention data, marking a discovery opportunity. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, slightly below observed, confirming efficacy. The protocol is a robust candidate for broader promotion.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Effectively eases overstimulation, especially for caregivers, though it’s not widely discovered yet.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) offers similar over‑stimulation relief. Qualitative feedback (n=21) shows most listeners notice improvement within 5‑10 min, though music ratings are mixed. YouTube views are very low (5). AutoGluon predicts 6.93, virtually identical to the observed mean. The protocol is stable but under‑exposed.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Reduces sensory overload quickly, but fewer people seem to have tried it.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) continues the over‑stimulation series. Qualitative data (n=23) highlight noticeable improvement, especially for sleep‑related use, with settle times around 5‑10 min. YouTube reach is limited (8 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.14, close to the observed mean. Performance is reliable across the sample.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Calms overstimulation well, particularly when used before sleep.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) targets anxiety & stress via a DESCENT arc. Qualitative 1‑minute responses (n=23) report rapid settling (≈5‑10 min) and predominantly positive music and rhythm ratings from self‑listeners. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views) with no retention data, indicating an undiscovered efficacy signal. AutoGluon predicts an anxiety‑zone score of 7.84, modestly above the observed mean, suggesting latent potential. Overall the protocol appears reliably effective with room for broader exposure.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners often feel calmer and less anxious after a short session, and many say it works quickly.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) also addresses anxiety & stress using DESCENT. Qualitative data (n=20) show mixed settle times (5‑20 min) and a split between modest and noticeable improvements, especially among professionals supporting clients. YouTube views are scarce (6) and retention metrics are unavailable. AutoGluon forecasts a comparable score of 6.95, aligning closely with the observed mean. The protocol demonstrates steady efficacy but may benefit from clearer targeting.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Provides steady anxiety relief, especially useful for professionals helping others, though it feels a bit less punchy than L1.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) follows the same anxiety DESCENT design. Listener comments (n=20) highlight noticeable improvement and settle times around 10 min, with a higher proportion of dependent‑support use. YouTube exposure is very low (4 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.23, slightly higher than observed, indicating modest untapped potential. Performance is consistent across the limited sample.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Works well for people supporting dependents, offering consistent anxiety reduction with a gentle pace.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) is a crisis DESCENT protocol. Qualitative feedback (n=24) indicates significant improvement for many, but settle times vary from 5‑10 min and adviser involvement is low (5/43). YouTube reach is negligible (6 views). AutoGluon predicts a much higher crisis score of 7.09, suggesting a gap between expectation and observed response. The protocol appears effective for some listeners but may need enhanced onboarding.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Helps people in crisis feel steadier within a few minutes, though experiences can vary.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) also serves acute distress. Qualitative data (n=16) reveal moderate to significant improvement with settle times around 5‑10 min. YouTube views remain low (4). AutoGluon forecasts a substantially higher crisis score of 7.96 and recommends cross‑bucket routing to low‑affect zones (X‑ME‑L1/L2), highlighting a possible mismatch in delivery. This protocol warrants investigation of routing logic.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Offers noticeable calm during acute distress, with many reporting quick improvement.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) completes the crisis set. Qualitative responses (n=19) note immediate or rapid improvement for many, though distribution is slightly bimodal. YouTube exposure is minimal (5 views). AutoGluon predicts a crisis score of 7.32 and suggests cross‑bucket routing, similar to H‑CR‑L2. The protocol shows promise but may benefit from refined listener segmentation.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Delivers fast relief for some in crisis, though responses differ across listeners.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) addresses sleep & rest via DESCENT. Qualitative responses (n=15) report noticeable improvement for most, with rapid settling (5‑10 min) during therapy sessions. YouTube views are minimal (4). AutoGluon predicts a higher sleep score of 7.54, indicating possible under‑recognition in the field. The protocol appears effective but invisible.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Supports deep rest and falling asleep, with many noticing improvement quickly.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) also targets sleep. Qualitative feedback (n=15) shows mixed improvement levels and occasional rhythm‑busyness concerns. YouTube exposure is low (5 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.16, aligning closely with observed performance. The protocol is stable yet under‑discovered.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Helps with sleep, especially for those in therapy, though some find the rhythm a bit busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) completes the sleep set. Qualitative data (n=22) indicate noticeable improvement for many, with settle times of 5‑10 min, often used for relaxation. YouTube views are very low (3). AutoGluon predicts 7.03, virtually matching the mean. The protocol is consistent but lacks visibility.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Provides reliable sleep aid, though it’s less talked about than other sleep options.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) targets focus & clarity via HOLD. Qualitative inputs (n=23) note noticeable improvement for many, though some report no change; settle times vary from immediate to 5 min. YouTube views are low (5). AutoGluon predicts 6.74, modestly above observed, indicating room for optimization.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Improves focus for many, though some need more time to notice the change.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) also supports focus. Qualitative feedback (n=17) reports moderate to significant improvement, especially during study or work, with settle times around 5‑10 min. YouTube reach is limited (4 views). AutoGluon predicts 6.96, slightly below the observed mean, suggesting strong real‑world performance.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Boosts concentration effectively, especially during study or work.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=strong) delivers the highest observed mean across the library. Qualitative responses (n=29) overwhelmingly report significant improvement in focus, with settle times typically 5‑10 min during study or chores. YouTube views are the highest in THRIVING (15) but still modest, and retention data are absent. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, very close to observed, confirming its standout efficacy. This protocol warrants amplified promotion.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Delivers the best focus enhancement across the library, helping users stay sharp.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) supports creative flow via HOLD. Qualitative responses (n=21) lean toward slight or moderate improvement, with immediate or 10‑minute settling. YouTube reach is negligible (2 views). AutoGluon predicts 6.76, essentially identical to observed. The protocol shows modest efficacy.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Gives a mild boost to creative flow, but the effect can be subtle.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) also targets creative flow. Qualitative feedback (n=15) includes notable love for rhythm and moderate improvement, settling within 5‑10 min. YouTube views remain low (2). AutoGluon forecasts 7.05, modestly higher than observed, suggesting potential under‑utilisation.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Offers a steadier creative lift, with listeners enjoying the rhythm more.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) is the top performer in the creative flow trio. Qualitative data (n=25) emphasize significant improvement and very settling rhythms, with settle times around 10 min. YouTube exposure is slightly higher (7 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.21, closely matching the observed high mean. This protocol appears both effective and promising for broader rollout.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Strongly enhances creativity, helping many break through blocks quickly.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) addresses maintenance & balance via HOLD. Qualitative data (n=23) highlight moderate improvement, often in therapy contexts, though settle times can extend to 20 min. YouTube exposure is minimal (3 views). AutoGluon predicts 6.82, slightly below observed, indicating decent real‑world performance. The protocol shows stable efficacy but limited discovery.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Supports mental balance well, though benefits may take a bit longer to feel.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) also serves balance. Qualitative input (n=16) reveals higher variance, with some users reporting no effect; settle times hover around 5‑10 min. YouTube views are low (4). AutoGluon predicts 6.71, essentially matching observed mean. The protocol’s larger variance suggests sub‑group differences.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Provides balance support with mixed results; some users feel more stable than others.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) is a smaller sample for balance. Qualitative feedback (n=19) reports noticeable improvement for most, though some did not settle. YouTube reach is also low (3). AutoGluon predicts 6.89, closely aligned. The protocol shows promise but needs more data.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Helps maintain balance, but more data are needed to confirm consistency.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) supports mood & energy via ASCENT. Qualitative data (n=22) show a split between significant and moderate improvement, especially among caregivers, with settle times often 10‑20 min. YouTube reach is modest (8). AutoGluon predicts a striking low‑affect score of 8.23, substantially higher than observed, indicating possible under‑exposure.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Lifts mood and energy for most listeners, especially caregivers, but it’s not widely known.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) also targets mood & energy. Qualitative responses (n=15) emphasize significant improvement and a preference for balanced rhythm. YouTube views are similar (8). AutoGluon predicts 8.18, notably above the observed mean, again suggesting discovery potential.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Provides reliable mood and energy uplift, with many noting strong improvements.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) is the lowest‑scoring mood & energy protocol. Qualitative feedback (n=25) reports moderate improvement for many, though some note no effect, and settle times are relatively short. YouTube exposure is very low (4). AutoGluon predicts 7.07, higher than observed, hinting at possible delivery or audience alignment issues.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Offers modest mood and energy benefits; some users feel little change.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) targets motivation via ASCENT. Qualitative comments (n=17) note mixed outcomes, with a few users reporting significant benefit and others little change. YouTube reach is modest (5). AutoGluon forecasts a high motivation score of 7.88, far above the observed mean, suggesting under‑delivery or audience mismatch.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivates some listeners, but many experience only modest benefits.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) also addresses motivation. Qualitative responses (n=21) indicate modest improvement, with settle times often extending to 20 min. YouTube views are slightly higher (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.24, again above observed, pointing to potential delivery gaps.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Boosts drive for a few, but overall effects are modest.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) is an outlier in motivation with a high mean but large variance. Qualitative feedback (n=17) is generally positive, though music opinions are mixed. YouTube exposure is low (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.29, close to observed, confirming its potency for a subset of listeners; further stratification is advisable.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Effectively energizes many users, though experiences vary widely.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) targets mind & clarity via DESCENT. Qualitative responses (n=25) are mostly positive, though two listeners reported worsening. YouTube views are moderate (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.05, modestly higher than observed, suggesting slight under‑performance. Overall the protocol is reasonably effective.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Generally clarifies thinking, though a few listeners felt it made things worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) also serves mind & clarity. Qualitative data (n=20) emphasize significant improvement and very settling rhythms. YouTube exposure is higher (12 views). AutoGluon predicts a strong mind‑clarity score of 7.54, notably above the observed mean, indicating potential under‑realized efficacy.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Strongly sharpens mental clarity, with most users feeling notably clearer.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) is the lowest performer in mind & clarity. Qualitative feedback (n=17) is polarized, with some reporting significant improvement and others no effect, suggesting a bimodal distribution. YouTube views are low (8). AutoGluon predicts a high mind‑clarity score of 7.538, a substantial divergence that flags a possible design‑population mismatch.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results are mixed—some love the clarity boost, others see no change.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The data suggest solid efficacy across all three series, with HEALING showing the highest consistency, THRIVING delivering strong mean scores, and TRANSFORMING exhibiting greater variability.
• HEALING protocols have the lowest overall standard deviation (≈1.77) and the largest sample (n=574).
• THRIVING shows the highest observed mean among individual protocols (T‑FC‑L3 = 7.63) despite moderate variance.
• TRANSFORMING displays the widest spread of outcomes (std≈1.87) consistent with its ASCENT design.
• AutoGluon predictions generally align with observed trends but highlight several under‑exposed protocols.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level patterns reflect their design intent while revealing actionable variances.
• HEALING: Consistently reliable across DESCENT entries, especially in Over‑stimulation and Sleep, with strong confidence tiers.
• THRIVING: Holds the highest point‑estimates, notably in Focus & Clarity (T‑FC‑L3) and Creative Flow (T‑CF‑L3), but shows some bimodal response distribution.
• TRANSFORMING: Shows expected high variance; ASCENT protocols such as X‑ME‑L1/L2 have high AutoGluon scores yet moderate observed means, indicating discovery gaps.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge from series‑level expectations and merit focused investigation.
• X‑MC‑L3 (n=10, mean=5.7) displays a markedly low mean and high dispersion, suggesting a subgroup effect or design mismatch.
• H‑SL‑L1 exhibits notable cross‑run drift (similarity = 0.61) and a modest AutoGluon under‑prediction, hinting at evolving response patterns.
• T‑FC‑L3 combines the highest observed mean with very low YouTube exposure (15 views), raising questions about discovery and reach.
• These anomalies suggest possible population splits, parameter drift, or exposure limitations.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted actions can sharpen efficacy signals and address identified gaps.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L3 – Increase promotional effort and platform placement to translate its high mean (7.63) into broader reach.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MD‑L3 – Explore a Level‑2 variant or extended hold phase, given its outlier high mean (7.15) with high variance.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑CR‑L2 – Investigate cross‑bucket routing to low‑affect zones (X‑ME‑L1/L2), as AutoGluon predicts a 7.96 score versus observed 6.52.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑OS‑L1 – Leverage its moderate YouTube reach (30 views) with targeted discovery to amplify a strong mean (7.15).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑SL‑L1 – Adjust early‑phase entrainment or groove parameters to address drift and align AutoGluon prediction (7.54) with observed outcomes.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals reveal both stability concerns and coherent groupings across protocols.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: Sixteen protocols show similarity below 0.75, notably H‑SL‑L1, T‑MB‑L1/L2, and X‑MC‑L2, indicating evolving response patterns.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Strong clusters include X‑ME‑L1/L2 (≈0.96 similarity) and the T‑CF trilogy (≈0.95), suggesting shared acoustic or functional traits.
• Confidence in embedding insights is moderate; limited historical runs (30 protocols) constrain robustness.

YouTube exposure is higher (12 views). AutoGluon predicts a strong mind‑clarity score of 7.54, notably above the observed mean, indicating potential under‑realized efficacy.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) is the lowest performer in mind & clarity. Qualitative feedback (n=17) is polarized, with some reporting significant improvement and others no effect, suggesting a bimodal distribution. YouTube views are low (8). AutoGluon predicts a high mind‑clarity score of 7.538, a substantial divergence that flags a possible design‑population mismatch.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: X-MD-L1 (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) targets motivation via ASCENT. Qualitative comments (n=17) note mixed outcomes, with a few users reporting significant benefit and others little change. YouTube reach is modest (5). AutoGluon forecasts a high motivation score of 7.88, far above the observed mean, suggesting under‑delivery or audience mismatch.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: X-MD-L2 (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) also addresses motivation. Qualitative responses (n=21) indicate modest improvement, with settle times often extending to 20 min. YouTube views are slightly higher (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.24, again above observed, pointing to potential delivery gaps.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: X-MD-L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) is an outlier in motivation with a high mean but large variance. Qualitative feedback (n=17) is generally positive, though music opinions are mixed. YouTube exposure is low (7). by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) supports mood & energy via ASCENT. Qualitative data (n=22) show a split between significant and moderate improvement, especially among caregivers, with settle times often 10‑20 min. YouTube reach is modest (8). by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) also targets mood & energy. by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) is the lowest‑scoring mood & energy protocol. by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Listeners often feel calmer and less anxious after a short session, and many say it works quickly.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Provides steady anxiety relief, especially useful for professionals helping others, though it feels a bit less punchy than L1.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Works well for people supporting dependents, offering consistent anxiety reduction with a gentle pace.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Helps people in crisis feel steadier within a few minutes, though experiences can vary.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Offers noticeable calm during acute distress, with many reporting quick improvement.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Delivers fast relief for some in crisis, though responses differ across listeners.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Effectively eases overstimulation, especially for caregivers, though it’s not widely discovered yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Reduces sensory overload quickly, but fewer people seem to have tried it.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Calms overstimulation well, particularly when used before sleep.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Supports deep rest and falling asleep, with many noticing improvement quickly.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Helps with sleep, especially for those in therapy, though some find the rhythm a bit busy.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Provides reliable sleep aid, though it’s less talked about than other sleep options.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Gives a mild boost to creative flow, but the effect can be subtle.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Offers a steadier creative lift, with listeners enjoying the rhythm more.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Strongly enhances creativity, helping many break through blocks quickly.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Improves focus for many, though some need more time to notice the change.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Boosts concentration effectively, especially during study or work.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Delivers the best focus enhancement across the library, helping users stay sharp.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Supports mental balance well, though benefits may take a bit longer to feel.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Provides balance support with mixed results; some users feel more stable than others.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Helps maintain balance, but more data are needed to confirm consistency.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Generally clarifies thinking, though a few listeners felt it made things worse.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Strongly sharpens mental clarity, with most users feeling notably clearer.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Results are mixed—some love the clarity boost, others see no change.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivates some listeners, but many experience only modest benefits.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Boosts drive for a few, but overall effects are modest.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Effectively energizes many users, though experiences vary widely.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Lifts mood and energy for most listeners, especially caregivers, but it’s not widely known.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Provides reliable mood and energy uplift, with many noting strong improvements.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Offers modest mood and energy benefits; some users feel little change.
